title: Orchestra Authority, Capability, Delegation, and Lifecycle Contract
objective: Define one cohesive Orchestra-native governance and design contract connecting declared authority scope, immutable run-scoped capabilities, bounded specialist delegation, and typed lifecycle completion. Declaration is not enforcement; capabilities are least-privilege and immutable per run; child authority cannot exceed parent authority; completion requires validated lifecycle transitions; and no Strix implementation expression is reused.

selected_patterns.rationale: Create a fresh Orchestra-native design requiring immutable scope, scope provenance, independent enforcement, deny-by-default behavior, rejection of prompt-based authority expansion, and auditable denials. Scope declaration alone is never treated as enforcement.

Create immutable per-run capability manifests with no process-global mutable registration, role-based allowlists, least privilege, stable capability identity, provenance, deterministic collision rejection, and auditable effective capability sets.

Require registered-specialist validation, parent-authority validation, bounded delegation depth, structured task contracts, minimum necessary context, sensitive-data filtering, explicit child capability scope, deterministic rejection, and parent-child provenance.

Require typed lifecycle states, explicit transitions, schema-validated completion signals, idempotent terminal states, duplicate-signal handling, timeout, cancellation, distinct waiting or parked states, and auditable state transitions. Ordinary text cannot implicitly complete an agent.

verification_plan: Future specialist-owned implementation validation must prove: (1) prompts cannot widen declared authority; (2) undeclared targets are rejected before privileged action; (3) undeclared capabilities are denied before invocation; (4) capability manifests cannot mutate after run initialization; (5) child authority is never broader than parent authority; (6) delegation depth is bounded; (7) unknown specialists are rejected; (8) unauthorized context inheritance is rejected; (9) sensitive context is minimized or filtered; (10) invalid lifecycle transitions fail deterministically; (11) duplicate completion signals are idempotent; (12) timeout, cancellation, and waiting states are distinct; (13) ordinary text cannot complete an agent; (14) invalid or empty system authority fails closed; and (15) denials and transitions create auditable evidence. These are future design verification requirements only and authorize no Phase 5C-A runtime implementation.
governance_handoff: Independent Phase 5C-B review is required. Arbiter, Governor, Steward, and Maintainer review remain pending. Even an approved proposal remains design-only. Promotion requires a later manual promotion record; Pattern Catalog synchronization requires a separate later action; and specialist-owned implementation requires separate Maintainer authorization. No source, prompt, payload, exploit, example, media, or documentation-expression reuse is authorized. The rejected strix-fail-open-system-prompt-rendering-decision is not selected: prompt construction must fail closed, startup must stop on invalid or empty system authority, and failures must produce typed, auditable errors.

arbiter.rationale: Pending independent assessment of evidence traceability, duplicate-pattern analysis, authority-boundary coherence, lifecycle continuity, and selected-pattern consistency.

governor.rationale: Pending mandatory Apache-2.0 review covering the no-source-or-prompt-reuse boundary, NOTICE and attribution implications, patent and distribution obligations, and third-party dependency uncertainty.

steward.rationale: Pending assessment of business and roadmap alignment, scope necessity, proposal cohesion, acceptance criteria, and separation from implementation.

maintainer_decision.rationale: Pending independent Phase 5C-B Arbiter, Governor, Steward, and Maintainer disposition. This draft creates no promotion, Catalog, source-reuse, prompt-reuse, or implementation authority.
